cards.back: A method for finding sub-game perfect equilibria in finite horizon games by recursively analyzing the game's payoffs in reverse chronological order.
A state in a game where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
A concept in game theory where a game is considered solved if the optimal strategy for each player is known and consistent across all sub-games of the original game.
A game where the number of stages or periods is finite, and the players' payoffs depend on the actions taken in each stage.
The process of solving a problem by breaking it down into smaller instances of the same problem, which are then solved recursively until the base case is reached.
